witnesses.description: The complex number i cycles through the fourth roots of unity.
Conjugation of a fixed product matches the product of conjugates.
A fixed rational Mobius transform sends 2 + i to 2/5 + 1/5 i.

checks.claim: The listed powers of i form an exact order-four unit-root cycle.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes each power with exact real-pair multiplication.

checks.claim: For the fixed pair, conjugate(z*w) equals conjugate(z)*conjugate(w).
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the product, conjugates, and product of conjugates exactly.

checks.claim: The listed rational Mobius-transform image is correct for z = 2 + i.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes (z - 1)/(z + 1), denominator norm, and image norm exactly.

checks.claim: Every square of a unit complex number has nonnegative real part.
checks.notes: The validator rejects the claim because i is unit length but i^2 has real part -1.

checks.claim: General complex-analysis theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: Cauchy-Riemann equations, Cauchy's theorem, residues, analytic continuation, and the fundamental theorem of algebra
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked by exact real-pair replay; analytic complex analysis remains a proof-assistant horizon.
